title: CPU Internal Structure
summary: Describes the internal components of a CPU: ALU, registers (general-purpose, PC, IR, MAR, MDR, temporary), and the control unit.
content_points: ALU performs calculations. Control unit commands other components and sets up timing.
PC: holds the address of the next instruction to execute; updated automatically or by branches.
IR: holds the code of the instruction currently being decoded.
MAR: memory address register – holds the address to be accessed. MDR: memory data register – holds data read from/written to memory.
Temporary registers: ALU operand and result registers.
learning_objectives: Name and describe the main internal components of a CPU
Explain the roles of PC, IR, MAR, and MDR
